112: The Richardson 112 needs no introduction — it's the trucker cap crews order by number, and the biggest single style in our shop's history: nearly 8,000 decorated across 211 orders. The pre-curved bill, structured front, and snapback fit made it the industry standard; a leatherette patch or clean front embroidery makes it yours. We stock the whole 112 family, including the R-Flex, printed mesh, and tactical variants.
112PL: The Richardson 112+ R-Flex adds a stretch-fit band to the legendary trucker — the 112 feel with a fitted hug that still adjusts. For crews already living in 112s, it's the comfort upgrade; patches and embroidery land exactly like they do on the original.
112PT: The Richardson Printed Tactical Trucker runs rugged printed patterns through the 112 formula — the same iconic profile with a tactical edge. Hunting outfits, contractors, and outdoor brands pick it; a leatherette or PVC patch is the natural finish.
112PM: The Richardson Printed Mesh Trucker patterns the mesh back of the classic 112 — subtle design where plain caps go quiet. Front-panel decoration stays identical to the original, so mixed orders across the 112 family keep one logo standard.
C402: The Port Authority Snapback Trucker C402 is the value trucker with a secret: it carries a 77% margin and looks nearly identical to caps twice its price. Nearly 1,500 decorated here, mostly in big event and crew batches. Foam front, mesh back, patch-and-embroidery friendly.
CP96: The Soft Brushed Canvas Cap holds our record for cap reorders — 286 separate orders and 2,600 units. It's the unstructured everyday canvas cap that suits every logo and every head; companies put it in new-hire kits and reorder it on autopilot.
C914: The Six-Panel Unstructured Twill Cap is the classic dad cap at one of the best margins on our wall — nearly 1,500 decorated. Low-profile, broken-in feel, and a front panel that flatters small, clean embroidery. The default cap for understated brands.
C114: The Digi Camo Snapback Trucker moves in serious volume — over 2,100 units here, mostly big-batch crew and event orders. Digital camouflage crown, solid front panel for legible decoration, snapback fit for one-size ordering.
CP77: The Brushed Twill Low Profile Cap is a quiet institution — over 1,400 decorated. Soft brushed twill, relaxed profile, and a price that works for giveaways without feeling like one. It embroiders cleanly and fits the way people expect a cap to fit.
C812: The Flexfit Mesh Back Cap pairs the fitted Flexfit band with a vented back — 69 separate orders here. It's the step-up trucker for companies that want fitted comfort across the crew in two sizes; the structured front takes dimensional embroidery beautifully.
C110: The Flexfit 110 Mesh Cap uses the 110 tech band — fitted feel, one adjustable size — under a breathable mesh back. It solves cap-sizing for whole-crew orders in a single SKU and keeps the structured front embroidery loves.
CP90L: The Fleece-Lined Knit Cap is the winter workhorse upgraded — the classic CP90 beanie with a fleece band inside. Over 900 decorated at a 75% margin; crews that work outside all winter notice the difference immediately.
CP85: The Cotton Twill Sandwich Bill Cap adds a contrast stripe in the bill edge — a small detail that makes team colors pop. Nearly 800 decorated; it's a classic company cap with just enough design to feel intentional.
C819: The Pigment Print Camouflage Cap moved nearly 700 units almost entirely in bulk runs — the washed, faded camo look crews and outdoor events grab by the box. Front embroidery in a solid thread keeps the logo readable over the pattern.
CP83: The Two-Tone Pigment-Dyed Cap has that sun-faded, garment-washed look straight out of the box — beach-town retail style at a 72% margin. It suits relaxed brands, taprooms, and anyone whose logo looks better on something broken-in.
C808: The Flexfit 210 Flat Bill delivers the fitted flat-brim look — the premium street silhouette with Flexfit's two-size fit. Nearly 400 decorated; it's the cap for younger crews and brands that want retail-wall style with an embroidered or patched front.
C302: The Flexfit Melange Mesh Back Trucker heathers the crown for a retail-styled trucker with fitted comfort. The melange texture makes tone-on-tone embroidery look especially sharp — a designer's pick in the cap program.
C925: The Digital Ripstop Camouflage Cap took a single 430-unit order — it's a big-batch favorite. Ripstop texture, digital camo, solid front panel for the logo. Crews, events, and outdoor brands order it in volume.
CP91: The Port Authority Beanie Cap is the value knit at a 79% margin — the straightforward beanie for winter crew orders and giveaways that still takes a tight embroidered cuff logo. Boxes of these leave our shop every November.
CP78: The Washed Twill Cap wears like it's been on the dashboard for a summer already — garment-washed softness with an unstructured fit. Six hundred-plus moved here in a few big runs; it's the broken-in classic for relaxed brands.
C909: The Americana Contrast Stitch Camouflage Cap layers flag-detail stitching over a camo crown — a statement cap for patriotic brands and crews. The solid front panel keeps embroidery legible over the pattern.
CP82: The Brushed Twill Cap is the traditional structured company cap — soft twill, classic profile, 74% margin. Four hundred-plus decorated; it's the safe, sharp default when a customer just wants 'a nice hat with the logo.'
C912: The Camouflage Cap with Air Mesh Back vents a camo trucker for hot-weather wear — 350-plus moved in a handful of batches. Hunting-adjacent brands and summer crews are the natural buyers; solid-thread embroidery up front.
C928: The Flexfit Wool Blend Cap is the dress cap of the fitted family — a smooth wool-blend face with Flexfit comfort. It photographs premium and embroiders sharper than twill; management tiers and retail-minded brands pick it.
C804: The Enhanced Visibility Cap with Camo Back is a genuinely clever combination — a hi-vis front panel for visibility, camo back for style. Road and utility crews grabbed 300 in two orders. The bright panel carries a bold dark logo perfectly.
C900: The R-Tek Stretch Fleece Beanie is the soft one — Port Authority's stretch fleece knit that slides under hard hats and hoods without bulk. Nearly 300 decorated across 27 orders at a 75% margin; winter crew orders love it.
PWU: The Garment-Washed Cap is the original relaxed classic — washed cotton, unstructured, instantly broken-in. It's been a quiet favorite for decades and still runs a 72% margin. Low-key embroidery suits it exactly.
BG518: The Insulated Lunch Tote took a single 750-unit order here — it's a company-gift natural. Real insulation, clean panel for an embroidered or printed logo, and daily use that keeps the brand on the breakroom table for years.
BG208: The Ridge Backpack is Port Authority's everyday pack — padded, organized, and priced for whole-team gifting. Two hundred-plus embroidered here; it's the new-hire-kit backpack that doesn't blow the budget.
PT400: The Grommeted Hemmed Towel is the golf-tournament staple — nearly 900 embroidered here, mostly in event runs. The grommet clips to a bag, the hemmed face takes a clean logo, and every foursome walks the course with your brand.
